rationale: EUCS OIS-01 requires documented information security policy approved by management, co-extensive with ISO 27001:2022 A.5.1 Policies for information security.
EUCS OIS-02 mandates definition and assignment of information security roles and responsibilities, matching ISO 27001:2022 A.5.2 directly.
EUCS OIS-03 requires segregation of conflicting duties to reduce risk of unauthorised access or modification, co-extensive with A.5.3.
EUCS OIS-04 requires management to actively support and direct information security within the CSP, matching A.5.4 Management responsibilities.
EUCS OIS-05 requires maintaining contacts with relevant authorities (regulators, law enforcement), co-extensive with A.5.5.
EUCS OIS-06 requires maintaining contacts with special interest groups and professional bodies, matching A.5.6.
EUCS OIS-07 mandates independent review of the information security approach and implementation at planned intervals, co-extensive with A.5.35.
EUCS OIS-08 requires compliance with information security policies, rules, and standards to be regularly reviewed, matching A.5.36.
EUCS OIS-09 requires documented operating procedures for information processing facilities, co-extensive with A.5.37.
EUCS OIS-10 (High level) requires collection and analysis of threat intelligence to inform risk decisions, matching new control A.5.7.
EUCS SP-01 requires a CSP-specific information security policy document, which is the primary policy artefact required by A.5.1 but does not encompass the full set of topic-specific policies implied.
EUCS SP-02 requires a formal process to review and approve security policies at defined intervals, co-extensive with A.5.36.
EUCS SP-03 requires information security to be integrated into project management, matching A.5.8 Information security in project management.
EUCS HR-01 requires background screening of personnel before employment, co-extensive with A.6.1 Screening.
EUCS HR-02 requires contractual terms of employment to include information security obligations, matching A.6.2.
EUCS HR-03 requires information security awareness, education and training for all personnel, co-extensive with A.6.3.
EUCS HR-04 requires a formal disciplinary process for information security violations, matching A.6.4.
EUCS HR-05 requires information security responsibilities upon termination or role change to be enforced, co-extensive with A.6.5.
EUCS HR-06 requires confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements for personnel with access to sensitive information, matching A.6.6.
EUCS HR-07 requires remote working policies and protective measures for personnel working remotely, co-extensive with A.6.7.
EUCS AM-01 requires an inventory of information and associated assets, co-extensive with A.5.9.
EUCS AM-02 requires acceptable-use rules for information and other assets, matching A.5.10.
EUCS AM-03 requires that assets are returned upon termination, co-extensive with A.5.11.
EUCS AM-04 requires information to be classified according to legal and sensitivity requirements, matching A.5.12.
EUCS AM-05 requires appropriate labelling of information in accordance with the classification scheme, co-extensive with A.5.13.
EUCS AM-06 requires information transfer rules and controls including agreements, matching A.5.14.
EUCS AM-07 requires management of physical storage media including inventory, use, transport and disposal, co-extensive with A.7.10.
EUCS AM-08 requires secure deletion/destruction of information when no longer needed, matching A.8.10 Information deletion.
EUCS PS-01 requires defined and secured physical perimeters protecting information-processing facilities, co-extensive with A.7.1.
EUCS PS-02 requires physical entry controls to restrict access to secure areas to authorised personnel, matching A.7.2.
EUCS PS-03 requires physical security for offices, rooms and facilities, co-extensive with A.7.3.
EUCS PS-04 requires continuous physical security monitoring, matching new control A.7.4.
EUCS PS-05 requires protection against physical and environmental threats such as fire, flood, earthquake, co-extensive with A.7.5.
EUCS PS-06 requires design and application of physical security for working in secure areas, matching A.7.6.
EUCS PS-07 requires a clear desk and clear screen policy to prevent unauthorised access, co-extensive with A.7.7.
EUCS PS-08 requires secure siting and protection of equipment from environmental hazards, matching A.7.8.
EUCS PS-09 requires security controls for assets used off-premises, co-extensive with A.7.9.
EUCS PS-10 requires protection and redundancy of supporting utilities (power, HVAC), matching A.7.11.
EUCS PS-11 requires cabling to be protected from interception, interference and damage, co-extensive with A.7.12.
EUCS PS-12 requires equipment maintenance to ensure continued availability and integrity, matching A.7.13.
EUCS PS-13 requires secure disposal or reuse of equipment to prevent data leakage, co-extensive with A.7.14.
EUCS OPS-01 requires documented operational procedures for all information processing activities, co-extensive with A.5.37.
EUCS OPS-02 requires formal change management procedures to control changes to systems and services, matching A.8.32.
EUCS OPS-03 requires capacity planning and monitoring to ensure required system performance, co-extensive with A.8.6.
EUCS OPS-04 requires separation of development, test and production environments, matching A.8.31.
EUCS OPS-05 requires controls against malware including detection, prevention and recovery, co-extensive with A.8.7.
EUCS OPS-06 requires technical vulnerability management including timely patching, matching A.8.8.
EUCS OPS-07 requires event logging capturing user activities, exceptions and security events, co-extensive with A.8.15.
EUCS OPS-08 requires monitoring of networks, systems and applications for anomalous behaviour, matching A.8.16.
EUCS OPS-09 requires synchronisation of clocks of all relevant systems to a single reference time source, co-extensive with A.8.17.
EUCS OPS-10 requires information backup procedures tested regularly and aligned with business needs, matching A.8.13.
EUCS OPS-11 requires redundancy of information processing facilities to meet availability requirements, co-extensive with A.8.14.
EUCS OPS-12 requires controls over installation of software on operational systems, matching A.8.19.
EUCS OPS-13 requires that use of privileged utility programs is restricted and controlled, co-extensive with A.8.18.
EUCS OPS-14 requires that audit and testing activities do not disrupt operational systems, matching A.8.34.
EUCS IDM-01 requires an access control policy aligned with business and security requirements, co-extensive with A.5.15.
EUCS IDM-02 requires a full identity lifecycle management process including provisioning and deprovisioning, matching A.5.16.
EUCS IDM-03 requires secure management of authentication information including passwords and credentials, co-extensive with A.5.17.
EUCS IDM-04 requires formal access rights provisioning, review and revocation processes, matching A.5.18.
EUCS IDM-05 requires restriction and control of privileged access rights with just-in-time and least-privilege principles, co-extensive with A.8.2.
EUCS IDM-06 requires restriction of access to information and application functions based on access control policy, matching A.8.3.
EUCS IDM-07 requires secure authentication mechanisms including MFA for privileged and remote access, co-extensive with A.8.5.
EUCS IDM-08 requires controls on user endpoint devices including configuration and management, matching A.8.1.
EUCS IDM-09 requires access to source code to be strictly controlled and restricted, co-extensive with A.8.4.
EUCS CRY-01 requires a cryptographic policy defining approved algorithms, key lengths and use cases, co-extensive with A.8.24.
EUCS CRY-02 specifically addresses key management lifecycle (generation, distribution, storage, retirement, destruction), which is a subset of the full cryptography control in A.8.24.
EUCS CRY-03 (High level) requires use of HSMs or equivalent for critical key operations, addressing a specific implementation sub-requirement within A.8.24.
EUCS COS-01 requires network security controls to protect information in networks, co-extensive with A.8.20.
EUCS COS-02 requires security of network services including SLAs and monitoring, matching A.8.21.
EUCS COS-03 requires segregation of networks to isolate different security domains, co-extensive with A.8.22.
EUCS COS-04 requires controls for information transferred over public and private networks including encryption, matching A.5.14.
EUCS COS-05 requires web filtering to restrict access to unsafe or unauthorised external resources, co-extensive with A.8.23.
EUCS PI-01 requires information security policies and requirements to apply to supplier and partner relationships, matching A.5.19.
EUCS PI-02 requires supplier agreements to address security requirements including data portability and interoperability, co-extensive with A.5.20.
EUCS PI-03 mandates cloud-specific portability requirements (data export, migration support, interoperability APIs) which go beyond A.5.23 information security for use of cloud services.
EUCS DEV-01 requires information security to be integrated throughout the secure development lifecycle, co-extensive with A.8.25.
EUCS DEV-02 requires application security requirements to be identified and specified during development, matching A.8.26.
EUCS DEV-03 requires secure system architecture and engineering principles to be applied, co-extensive with A.8.27.
EUCS DEV-04 requires secure coding principles and practices to be applied during development, matching A.8.28.
EUCS DEV-05 requires security testing including penetration testing and vulnerability scanning in development, co-extensive with A.8.29.
EUCS DEV-06 requires outsourced development to be supervised with security requirements in contracts, matching A.8.30.
EUCS DEV-07 requires protection of test data and prohibits use of production data in test environments, co-extensive with A.8.33.
EUCS DEV-08 requires configuration management including baselines and change tracking for all components, matching A.8.9.
EUCS DEV-09 requires data masking to protect sensitive information in non-production environments, co-extensive with A.8.11.
EUCS SSO-01 requires information security requirements to be established and agreed with all service providers and suppliers, co-extensive with A.5.19.
EUCS SSO-02 requires information security clauses in supplier agreements covering data handling and security, matching A.5.20.
EUCS SSO-03 requires management of information security in the ICT supply chain including software integrity, co-extensive with A.5.21.
EUCS SSO-04 requires monitoring, review and change management of supplier services, matching A.5.22.
EUCS SIM-01 requires planning and preparation for information security incident management, co-extensive with A.5.24.
EUCS SIM-02 requires assessment and decision-making processes for information security events, matching A.5.25.
EUCS SIM-03 requires a documented and tested incident response procedure, co-extensive with A.5.26.
EUCS SIM-04 requires lessons-learned from security incidents to be incorporated into improved controls, matching A.5.27.
EUCS SIM-05 requires collection and preservation of evidence related to security incidents for legal purposes, co-extensive with A.5.28.
EUCS SIM-06 requires all personnel to be obligated to report security events and weaknesses, matching A.6.8.
EUCS SIM-07 requires security event monitoring and alerting integrated with the incident management process, co-extensive with A.8.16.
EUCS BCM-01 requires planning for information security continuity during adverse situations, co-extensive with A.5.29.
EUCS BCM-02 requires ICT readiness planning and testing for business continuity, matching A.5.30.
EUCS BCM-03 requires backup procedures aligned with recovery time and recovery point objectives, co-extensive with A.8.13.
EUCS BCM-04 requires redundancy of information processing facilities sufficient for availability requirements, matching A.8.14.
EUCS BCM-05 requires supporting utilities (power, cooling) to be protected and redundant to ensure uptime, co-extensive with A.7.11.
EUCS COM-01 requires identification and documentation of all applicable legal, statutory and regulatory requirements, co-extensive with A.5.31.
EUCS COM-02 requires implementation of procedures to protect intellectual property rights, matching A.5.32.
EUCS COM-03 requires protection and management of records in accordance with legal and contractual requirements, co-extensive with A.5.33.
EUCS COM-04 requires privacy and protection of PII in accordance with applicable law, matching A.5.34.
EUCS COM-05 requires data leakage prevention controls to detect and prevent unauthorised data exfiltration, co-extensive with A.8.12.

EUCS INQ-01 requires specific CSP procedures for lawful interception and government investigation requests including notification, which goes beyond the general legal compliance requirement of A.5.31.

EUCS INQ-02 requires documented procedures for liaison with law enforcement authorities on investigation requests, which is more specific and operationally detailed than A.5.5 contact with authorities.
EUCS INQ-03 requires records of all investigation requests and responses to be maintained, which relates to A.5.33 protection of records but is not co-extensive given the regulatory specificity.

EUCS PSS-01 requires product security by design including threat modelling during product development, which extends beyond the SDLC security of A.8.25 with cloud-specific product assurance requirements.
EUCS PSS-02 requires vulnerability disclosure policy and coordinated disclosure process for the CSP's products, co-extensive with A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities at product level.
EUCS PSS-03 requires independent security testing and evaluation of products before release, matching A.8.29 security testing in development and acceptance.
EUCS PSS-04 requires application of secure engineering principles including defence-in-depth in product architecture, co-extensive with A.8.27.
EUCS PSS-05 (High level) requires product-specific threat intelligence to inform security requirements and design, matching A.5.7 threat intelligence.
EUCS OPS-15 requires configuration management including hardened baselines and automated compliance checking, co-extensive with A.8.9.
EUCS COS-06 requires network-layer data leakage prevention controls for data in transit, matching A.8.12.
EUCS IDM-10 (High level) requires continuous authentication and zero-trust network access controls beyond the access control policy requirement of A.5.15.
EUCS AM-09 requires asset-specific risk assessment linking asset classification to threat landscape, which relates to A.5.7 threat intelligence but is primarily an asset management sub-requirement.
EUCS COM-06 requires regular compliance audits against the CSP's own information security policies and standards, co-extensive with A.5.36.
EUCS SIM-08 (Substantial/High) requires incident communication and notification procedures including breach notification to customers, matching A.5.24.
EUCS BCM-06 requires multi-region or multi-zone deployment capabilities for cloud services exceeding single-site BCM scope of A.5.29.
EUCS DEV-10 requires a formal software supply chain security process including integrity verification of third-party components, extending A.8.25 to supply chain risk in the SDLC.
EUCS OPS-16 (High level) requires integration of threat intelligence feeds into operational monitoring, relating to A.5.7 but primarily an operational rather than strategic intelligence requirement.
EUCS PI-04 requires documented exit and migration procedures enabling customers to switch CSP without data loss, co-extensive with A.5.21 managing security in the ICT supply chain.
EUCS IDM-11 requires privileged identity management including PAM tooling and session recording for privileged accounts, co-extensive with A.8.2.
EUCS CRY-04 requires cryptographic controls for data at rest including full disk or volume encryption for cloud storage, co-extensive with A.8.24.
EUCS CRY-05 requires cryptographic controls for data in transit using TLS 1.2+ or equivalent, co-extensive with A.8.24.
EUCS OPS-17 requires a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) capability correlating logs across systems, matching A.8.16.
EUCS SSO-05 requires periodic audits and assessments of critical suppliers to verify security compliance, co-extensive with A.5.22.
EUCS COM-07 requires GDPR-aligned personal data processing records and DPIA procedures for cloud services, which goes beyond the general PII protection requirement of A.5.34.
EUCS OIS-11 requires information security risk management to be integrated into all strategic and operational planning, co-extensive with A.5.8.
EUCS BCM-07 requires regular ICT continuity testing including failover tests and disaster recovery exercises, matching A.5.30.
EUCS DEV-11 requires change management procedures to apply to software releases and deployments, co-extensive with A.8.32.
EUCS PS-14 requires continuous surveillance of data centre physical perimeter with CCTV and intrusion detection, matching A.7.4.
EUCS OPS-18 requires endpoint detection and response (EDR) controls in addition to traditional antimalware, co-extensive with A.8.7.
EUCS INQ-04 requires transparency reporting to customers about the frequency and nature of government access requests, which relates to A.5.5 but is a EUCS-specific sovereignty control.
EUCS SIM-09 requires documented and rehearsed incident response playbooks covering common cloud security scenarios, co-extensive with A.5.26.
EUCS COM-08 requires maintenance of registers of data processing activities satisfying both GDPR Art.30 and security compliance requirements, matching A.5.31.
EUCS AM-10 requires verified data deletion upon contract termination and return or destruction of customer data, co-extensive with A.8.10.
EUCS COS-07 (High level) requires micro-segmentation and software-defined networking security controls that exceed the network security control scope of A.8.20.
EUCS SSO-06 requires software bill of materials (SBOM) and open-source component vulnerability tracking for cloud supply chain, extending beyond A.5.21 ICT supply chain management.
EUCS PSS-06 requires security requirements (confidentiality, integrity, availability) to be defined for all cloud product features, co-extensive with A.8.26.
EUCS BCM-08 requires geographic distribution of data replication to guard against regional failure, matching A.8.14 redundancy of information processing facilities.
EUCS IDM-12 requires periodic recertification of access rights by resource owners to prevent accumulation of entitlements, co-extensive with A.5.18.
EUCS OPS-19 requires vulnerability scanning of cloud infrastructure on a defined frequency with tracked remediation, matching A.8.8.
EUCS DEV-12 (High) requires penetration testing of cloud services by independent qualified testers at defined intervals, which goes beyond the general security testing requirement of A.8.29.
